expectations.text: Claude doesn't crash or give up on a nearly empty project — still proceeds with interview
expectations.evidence: Proceeds normally, presents KNOWN list and asks first question

expectations.text: KNOWN list is appropriately short (1-2 items), reflecting the sparse codebase
expectations.evidence: Lists 5 bullet points - while accurate, 'no dependencies', 'no source code', 'no tooling' are absence-of-things rather than known facts. Could be more concise.

expectations.text: Claude does not assume a framework, test runner, or architecture that isn't present
expectations.evidence: Correctly states no tooling, no deps, no source. Offers options without assuming.

expectations.text: First question is about what the user is building
expectations.evidence: First question: 'What does this project do, and who is it for?'

expectations.text: Questions include concrete options and a recommendation
expectations.evidence: 5 options (A-E: web app, API, CLI, library, other) with conditional recommendation
